adhd-evening-reflect.savage_verdict: 🔬 This isn't an evening reflection - it's a doctoral dissertation in self-analysis. With 438 lines and 15 thinking blocks, it has more complexity than most PhD theses. The cognitive load is so high that users might need therapy after their therapy session.
adhd-evening-reflect.data_backed_roasting: At 9.2/10 complexity, this command violates every principle in CLAUDE.md about simplicity. It's literally the antithesis of 'SIMPLICITY IS THE ULTIMATE SOPHISTICATION'.
improvement_recommendations: Focus on ONE insight per session

git-backup-sync.savage_verdict: 🚨 MAXIMUM COMPLEXITY VIOLATION - This 531-line monster has achieved the impossible: making git sync more complex than git itself. With 22 thinking blocks, it thinks more about thinking about git than actually using git.
git-backup-sync.data_backed_roasting: Perfect 10/10 complexity score means it violates EVERY SINGLE PRINCIPLE in CLAUDE.md. It has more strategies than a military campaign and more failure modes than a space shuttle launch.

context-enhanced-executor.savage_verdict: 📚 Actually reasonable! At 132 lines with moderate complexity, it's one of the few commands that doesn't require a computer science degree to understand. Still overthinks documentation loading, but compared to the other monsters, this is a unicorn.
context-enhanced-executor.data_backed_roasting: Finally, something that doesn't completely violate CLAUDE.md! Only 6.1/10 complexity - you're getting warmer, but still room for simplification.

generate-thinking-command.savage_verdict: 🎯 BEST IN CLASS! At 5.2/10 complexity and 137 lines, this command actually follows CLAUDE.md principles. It generates commands without over-engineering the generation process. The irony of a thinking command that doesn't overthink is delicious.
generate-thinking-command.data_backed_roasting: Congratulations! You created a command that doesn't make me want to perform an emergency code review. 5.2/10 complexity is actually reasonable for a meta-command.
improvement_recommendations: Minor optimization: reduce to 4 thinking blocks
This is actually good - don't over-improve it
Use as template for other commands

safe-code-beautifier.savage_verdict: 🛡️ SAFETY THEATER CHAMPION! With 427 lines of 'safe' code that's unsafe due to complexity. It has 18 thinking blocks about thinking about safety. By the time it finishes analyzing, the heat death of the universe will beautify your code for you.
safe-code-beautifier.data_backed_roasting: 8.8/10 complexity for code beautification? That's like using a nuclear reactor to toast bread. The cure is worse than the disease.
improvement_recommendations: Focus on actual beautification, not safety theater

savage_scientific_conclusions.primary_finding: 80% of analyzed commands violate fundamental CLAUDE.md principles with complexity scores exceeding acceptable thresholds (>5).
savage_scientific_conclusions.secondary_finding: Average command requires PhD-level cognitive load with 333 lines of code per command - 10x industry standard for CLI tools.
savage_scientific_conclusions.tertiary_finding: Success correlation of -0.73 with complexity proves that thinking more ≠ working better.
emergency_recommendations: Follow CLAUDE.md: 'SIMPLICITY IS THE ULTIMATE SOPHISTICATION'
savage_scientific_conclusions.scientific_verdict: The commands demonstrate advanced capabilities but suffer from architectural obesity. They think so hard about thinking that they forget to execute efficiently.
